title: drymaria diandra
intro: Drymaria diandra, also known as creeping drymary or pinkling, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Drymaria diandra include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used topically to treat minor skin issues and promote wound healing.
